summary:
The patient exhibits classical signs of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), including hyperandrogenism manifesting as acne and hirsutism, oligomenorrhea every 45 days, and confirmed insulin resistance from an elevated two-hour glucose level exceeding 140 mg/dL. These findings collectively point to PCOS as the primary diagnosis due to their alignment with its core pathological features.  

Note: This summary focuses solely on clinically relevant aspects related to diagnosing PCOS based on available data.
